title: Authenticated Scope, Secrets, SSRF, Untrusted Content, and Privacy · Execution Trace: Sensitive Retention
summary: Credentials enter config files, cookies cross accounts, GET endpoints mutate state, extracted text reaches HTML and spreadsheets, and sensitive state persists in shared caches. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go sensitive retention increment in least-authority secure crawl boundary. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Minimize, classify, encrypt where required, restrict, expire, delete, and audit sensitive values across all derivatives.
Use safe text sinks, context encoding, reviewed sanitization, CSV formula defense, path confinement, and structured control-safe logs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Sensitive Retention · Theory Sensitive Retention
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 4315 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to defend authenticated authority, secrets, request effects, output sinks, retention, and derived privacy state; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Build Sensitive Retention evidence · Theory Sensitive Retention
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-sensitive-retention-14pxvf" that uses case 4596 to defend authenticated authority, secrets, request effects, output sinks, retention, and derived privacy state. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
